mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within three months, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and industrial production indicate weakening aggregate demand and supply chain bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and drive negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows insignificant coefficients (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter increase in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not statistically higher than the median return during flat or declining INDPRO periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings balances indicate households are accumulating buffers against uncertainty, which sustains consumer spending and supports equity valuations over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS changes and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 12-month rolling window, indicating the savings buffer no longer translates to market support.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently high to compress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive average 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, or the signal's information coefficient drops below zero over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate signals a cooling labor market where job creation slows faster than joblessness rises, prompting recession fears and driving negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth decelerates while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows insignificant coefficients (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth combined with rising inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that erode corporate profit margins and compress equity valuations over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% MoM
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the stagflation signal, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
